gestures.mdmPrompt: The tutor stands tall, leans slightly forward, and opens both arms wide while explaining multiplication.
The tutor raises their right arm, points towards the problem on the board, and invites learners to participate.
The tutor shifts weight onto one leg, raising both hands to count groups while asking how many are being added.
The tutor leans slightly to the left, using their left hand to count while clearly posing the question about addition.
The tutor stands confidently, opens arms wide, and emphasizes the question about multiplication with inviting gestures.
The tutor shifts to the right, raises their right hand with confidence, and re-explains multiplication through gestural emphasis.
The tutor points with their right hand toward the new problem, encouraging learners to engage with excitement.
The tutor uses both hands to count groups enthusiastically while asking how many groups learners should add.
The tutor leans forward slightly, using their left hand to count while asking about the addition of groups.
The tutor stands firm, opens arms wide for emphasis, and invites learners to retry the original multiplication question.
